description:
## Problem

The Rig Update function only updates the  field:



If a user changes , the Update:
1. Does NOT apply the change to the actual rig
2. Still updates state to claim the new value was set
3. User thinks change applied, but it didn't

## Impact

Silent partial failure. Users believe all their changes are applied when only some are. The  attribute appears mutable but isn't.

## Expected Behavior

Either:
1. Update should apply max_polecats changes via , OR
2. max_polecats should have RequiresReplace plan modifier, OR  
3. Update should return an error if max_polecats changes

## Files
- internal/gastown/rig/resource.go (lines 198-213)
title: Rig Update silently ignores max_polecats changes - partial update failure

description:
## Context
Provider: github.com/kybernetes-systems/terraform-provider-gastown
Registry: registry.terraform.io/kybernetes-systems/gastown

## Prerequisites
tfgt-nqg and tfgt-6od must be closed (all acceptance tests passing cleanly) before this issue is worked.

## Steps

### 1. Generate docs
Install tfplugindocs:
  go install github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-docs/cmd/tfplugindocs@latest

Run from repo root:
  tfplugindocs generate

This reads schema descriptions from provider.go and resource Schema() methods and generates docs/ markdown. Verify docs/ is populated for gastown_hq, gastown_rig, gastown_crew.

Ensure each resource Schema attribute has a Description field set — tfplugindocs uses these. Patch any missing descriptions before generating.

### 2. Add examples
Create testdata/examples/ directory with minimal working HCL examples for each resource:
  examples/resources/gastown_hq/resource.tf
  examples/resources/gastown_rig/resource.tf
  examples/resources/gastown_crew/resource.tf

### 3. Linters
Run:
  go vet ./...
  golangci-lint run   (install if not present: https://golangci-lint.run/usage/install/)

Fix any issues before proceeding.

### 4. GitHub release workflow
Create .github/workflows/release.yml using the standard goreleaser + Terraform registry signing pattern:
  - Trigger: push to tags matching v*
  - Uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action
  - Requires: GPG_PRIVATE_KEY and PASSPHRASE secrets set in the repo
  - Signs the release artifacts so the Terraform registry accepts them

Reference: https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/providers-plugin-framework/providers-plugin-framework-release-publish

### 5. Tag and publish
  git tag v0.1.0
  git push origin v0.1.0

Verify the release appears on the Terraform registry under kybernetes-systems/gastown.

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/ directory populated with generated provider and resource documentation
- go vet and golangci-lint pass clean
- GitHub Actions release workflow present and functional
- v0.1.0 visible on registry.terraform.io/kybernetes-systems/gastown
title: Phase 7: Documentation & release

close_reason: Deacon Patrol completed: Cleaned up orphaned dolt process on port 3917 (PID 860247) from acceptance test /tmp/TestHQResource_Create_callsGtInstall*. Confirmed Town Dolt running correctly on port 3957. GT_DOLT_PORT configuration issue resolved - port 3917 was a stale test artifact, not a configuration error.
description:
Environment GT_DOLT_PORT is set to 3917, but no Dolt server is listening on that port. Town Dolt runs on 3957. This causes circuit-breaker failures in:
- gt patrol report
- gt escalate  
- Any gt command requiring Dolt JSON serialization

Basic bd commands work, suggesting a port/configuration mismatch rather than Dolt outage.

Root cause: Missing Dolt instance for port 3917 or incorrect GT_DOLT_PORT override.

Impact: Refinery patrol cycles cannot complete with gt patrol report (but can work around with manual bd updates).
title: GT_DOLT_PORT configuration mismatch: 3917 server not running

2. internal/testutil/git-server.go

3. internal/gastown/rig/resource.go

   - This ensures max_polecats is properly applied to rigs

4. internal/provider/acceptance_test.go

Acceptance criteria:
- All unit tests pass
- max_polecats is now properly set via gt config
- Acceptance tests explicitly set max_polecats = 0
- SafeRunner available for future test hardening

Note: Full acceptance test run requires Dolt server (separate issue tfgt-1p4)
description:
## Problem
Running the acceptance test suite (TF_ACC=1 go test ./...) spawns real Claude processes as polecat workers because the tests create live Gas Town rigs. This is expensive, slow, non-deterministic, and leaves orphaned processes on the host.

ADR 0011 (docs/adr/0011-no-polecats-in-tests.md) documents the decision to prevent this — read it before making changes.

## Fix
Acceptance tests must not trigger real polecat/Claude spawning. Verify and enforce the following:

1. Read docs/adr/0011-no-polecats-in-tests.md for the agreed approach.

2. Ensure gastown_rig test fixtures set max_polecats = 0 or equivalent to suppress worker spawning during tests.

3. If gt itself spawns workers on rig creation regardless of config, the test harness must mock or stub the gt binary (see internal/testutil/) rather than invoking the real one for rig lifecycle tests.

4. After any test run, verify no orphaned `claude` or polecat processes remain:
     ps aux | grep -E 'claude|polecat'

## Acceptance criteria
- TF_ACC=1 go test ./... completes without spawning any Claude or polecat processes
- No orphaned processes remain after test suite exits
- ADR 0011 approach is correctly implemented, not just documented
title: Acceptance tests must not spawn live Claude/polecat processes

description:
## Context
Provider: github.com/kybernetes-systems/terraform-provider-gastown
File: internal/gastown/hq/resource.go

## Problem
The current Dolt port selection in acceptance tests uses a time-based offset to avoid port collisions between parallel test runs. This is racy and causes flaky test failures when two test instances happen to select the same port.

Current code (approximately):
  port := 3307
  if strings.Contains(hqPath, "gt2") { port = 3308 }
  port += (int(time.Now().Unix()) % N)

## Fix
Replace the time-based approach with OS-assigned ports using net.Listen:

  import "net"

  func getFreePort() (int, error) {
    l, err := net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0")
    if err != nil { return 0, err }
    defer l.Close()
    return l.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr).Port, nil
  }

Call getFreePort() during HQ creation in TF_ACC mode and write the result to the Dolt config. This guarantees a free port without any timing dependency.

Note: also verify that post-test destroy does not fail due to Dolt circuit breaker being open. If it does, ensure the HQ destroy sequence stops the Dolt server cleanly before removing state.

## Acceptance criteria
- Running TF_ACC=1 go test ./... multiple times in rapid succession does not produce port conflict errors
- Test teardown (destroy) completes without Dolt circuit breaker errors
title: Stabilize acceptance tests: replace flaky Dolt port selection with OS-assigned ports

description:
## Problem

The HQ resource's Update function is completely empty:



This means if a user changes , usage: git [-v | --version] [-h | --help] [-C <path>] [-c <name>=<value>]
           [--exec-path[=<path>]] [--html-path] [--man-path] [--info-path]
           [-p | --paginate | -P | --no-pager] [--no-replace-objects] [--no-lazy-fetch]
           [--no-optional-locks] [--no-advice] [--bare] [--git-dir=<path>]
           [--work-tree=<path>] [--namespace=<name>] [--config-env=<name>=<envvar>]
           <command> [<args>]

These are common Git commands used in various situations:

start a working area (see also: git help tutorial)
   clone     Clone a repository into a new directory
   init      Create an empty Git repository or reinitialize an existing one

work on the current change (see also: git help everyday)
   add       Add file contents to the index
   mv        Move or rename a file, a directory, or a symlink
   restore   Restore working tree files
   rm        Remove files from the working tree and from the index

examine the history and state (see also: git help revisions)
   bisect    Use binary search to find the commit that introduced a bug
   diff      Show changes between commits, commit and working tree, etc
   grep      Print lines matching a pattern
   log       Show commit logs
   show      Show various types of objects
   status    Show the working tree status

grow, mark and tweak your common history
   branch    List, create, or delete branches
   commit    Record changes to the repository
   merge     Join two or more development histories together
   rebase    Reapply commits on top of another base tip
   reset     Reset current HEAD to the specified state
   switch    Switch branches
   tag       Create, list, delete or verify a tag object signed with GPG

collaborate (see also: git help workflows)
   fetch     Download objects and refs from another repository
   pull      Fetch from and integrate with another repository or a local branch
   push      Update remote refs along with associated objects

'git help -a' and 'git help -g' list available subcommands and some
concept guides. See 'git help <command>' or 'git help <concept>'
to read about a specific subcommand or concept.
See 'git help git' for an overview of the system., or :
1. Terraform detects the change and plans an update
2. Update runs and succeeds (no error returned)
3. State is updated to reflect new values
4. Actual HQ remains unchanged
5. Subsequent plans show no changes (drift is now permanent)

## Impact

This is a silent data consistency failure. Users believe their configuration changes are applied when they are not. The state file lies about the actual infrastructure state.

## Expected Behavior

Either:
1. Update should apply changes to the HQ (if gt CLI supports it), OR
2. Update should return an error: "Update not supported - resource must be recreated", OR
3. All attributes should have RequiresReplace plan modifier to force recreation

## Current Workaround

Users must taint and recreate the HQ resource for any attribute changes.

## Files
- internal/gastown/hq/resource.go (lines 197-198)
title: HQ Update function silently does nothing - causes permanent drift

description:
## Problem

The ADR 0007 claims all three resources implement ImportState:

> All three resources implement ImportState. Import IDs are:
> - gastown_hq: Absolute path to HQ root
> - gastown_rig: <rig_name>
> - gastown_crew: <rig_name>/<crew_name>

However, examining the code:
- ✅ gastown_hq: Has  and  method
- ❌ gastown_rig: NO ImportState implementation
- ❌ gastown_crew: NO ImportState implementation

## Impact

Users following the documentation will fail when attempting to import rigs or crew resources. Terraform will report that import is not supported for these resource types.

## Fix

Either:
1. Implement ImportState for rig and crew resources, OR
2. Update ADR 0007 to correctly state that only HQ supports import

## Files
- internal/gastown/rig/resource.go (missing ImportState)
- internal/gastown/crew/resource.go (missing ImportState)
- docs/adr/0007-import-support.md (incorrect documentation)
title: Documentation claims all resources support import, but only HQ implements ImportState

description:
## Context
Provider: github.com/kybernetes-systems/terraform-provider-gastown
File: internal/gastown/rig/resource.go

## Problem 1: prefix attribute is unknown after apply
gastown_rig has a computed `prefix` attribute. Currently Create calls `gt rig add <name> <repo>` but never reads the prefix back from gt. After apply, Terraform sees prefix as unknown, causing a perpetual plan diff and acceptance test failure.

## Fix 1
After the `gt rig add` call succeeds in Create, immediately call:
  gt rig list --json
or:
  gt config get <name> prefix
Parse the output to extract prefix for the newly created rig, then set plan.Prefix before writing state with resp.State.Set(ctx, &plan).

The same read-back must happen in the Read method — call gt to get current prefix and populate state.Prefix.

## Problem 2: rig names with hyphens rejected by gt
gt CLI rejects rig names containing hyphens (e.g. "drift-rig", "mirror-rig"). Acceptance test fixtures use hyphenated names, causing gt to return an error during Create.

## Fix 2
In internal/provider/acceptance_test.go (and any other test files), rename all rig name fixtures:
  drift-rig  -> driftrig
  mirror-rig -> mirrorrig
  (etc.)
No changes to production code required for this fix.

## Acceptance criteria
- TF_ACC=1 go test ./... passes with no unknown attribute errors for prefix
- No "invalid characters" errors from gt during acceptance tests
- gastown_rig lifecycle test (create → read → drift → destroy) passes cleanly
title: Fix remaining acceptance test failures: prefix unknown and invalid rig names

description:
## Problem

Acceptance and unit tests are spawning Gas Town daemon processes (mayor, deacon, boot, witness) that are never cleaned up. After test runs, these processes remain running indefinitely.

### Evidence

Currently 12 orphaned processes from test runs:

```
PID      CWD
1210692  /tmp/TestAcc_DriftScenario139813555/001/gt-drift/mayor
1210699  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon/dogs/boot/deacon
1210746  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon
1210758  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon/dogs/boot/mayor
1210773  /tmp/TestHQResource_Create_callsGtInstall1762424197/001/gt/deacon
1210775  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/mayor
1210783  /tmp/TestAcc_DriftScenario139813555/001/gt-drift/deacon
1221173  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon/dogs/boot/witness
1225031  /tmp/TestAcc_DriftScenario139813555/001/gt-drift/deacon/dogs/boot
1233097  /tmp/TestHQResource_Read_idempotent175059459/001/gt/deacon/dogs/boot
1233890  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon/dogs/boot/deacon/dogs/boot
1239096  /tmp/TestAcc_FullLifecycle2698441245/001/gt-lifecycle/deacon/dogs/boot
```

### Root Cause

1. Tests create HQs using `gastown_hq` resource
2. Running `gt` commands spawns daemon processes (deacon patrol, mayor, boot triage, witness)
3. Test cleanup only calls `gt rig stop` and `gt rig dock` - but doesn't stop the HQ daemon processes
4. The `AssertNoPolecat` function only checks for polecats, not the daemon infrastructure

### Impact

- Resource exhaustion (each Claude process uses significant memory)
- Test isolation issues (orphaned processes may interfere with subsequent runs)
- System pollution

### Proposed Solution

1. Add `testutil.AssertNoDaemons(t, hqPath)` that verifies no mayor/deacon/boot/witness processes for a given HQ
2. Call this in `t.Cleanup` after HQ creation in all tests
3. If daemons are found, attempt graceful shutdown via `gt deacon stop`, `gt mayor stop`, etc.
4. If graceful shutdown fails, force kill the processes

### Related

- ADR 0011: Tests Must Not Spawn Polecats (we need similar protection for daemon processes)
title: Test suite leaves orphaned Gas Town daemon processes behind
